title: Shizuoka governor approves construction of Chuo Line maglev train
summary: After nearly a decade of stagnation, the only remaining section to complete the maglev line connecting Tokyo and Nagoya has been authorized, with its opening expected in 2036 or later.

title: Japanese 10-year bond yield reaches 2.85%, highest in 30 years
summary: The rate hike is due to concerns over increased fiscal spending, sending sovereign debt to levels not seen since the 1990s.

title: Belgium eliminates United States from World Cup as controversy surrounds Balogun and refereeing
summary: The round of 16 match ends with a Belgian victory, the elimination of all host countries, and controversy over the suspension waived for Balogun and a possible unsanctioned red card.

title: NATO summit opens with US pressure on European allies to increase defense spending
summary: President Trump criticizes the lack of European contribution and demands an increase in military spending, while allies try to justify their efforts.

title: China conducts test launch of SLBM ballistic missile to demonstrate nuclear deterrence capability
summary: The military exercise, which the United States calls 'grave global concern', seeks to strengthen nuclear counterstrike capability against Washington.

title: Former nursing home employee sentenced to 20 years for injecting air into IVs of two patients, but acquitted of murder
summary: The woman, who worked at a facility for the elderly in Ibaraki, was found guilty of one count of homicide, while another murder charge was dismissed.

title: Shohei Ohtani one home run away from reaching 300 in Major League Baseball
summary: The Japanese star hit his 19th home run of the season, a two-run shot that turned the game around, putting him on the verge of the historic milestone.

title: Rapper D.O arrested for possession of marijuana and cocaine in Japan
summary: The artist, known for his career in hip-hop, denied being the owner of the drugs found in his possession.

title: Amnesty International Japan dismisses secretary general for workplace harassment
summary: After an internal investigation confirmed the complaints of six employees, the organization decided to remove the senior official.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi and Ishin party leader meet to discuss reduction in number of Lower House seats
summary: The meeting between the prime minister and Hirofumi Yoshimura addresses possible scenarios for political reform amid confrontation between the ruling party and the opposition.

title: Japan's composite index of economic indicators rises for third consecutive month
summary: The improvement reflects a rebound in industrial production, although the economy shows mixed signals.

title: Personal data leaks in Japan decrease 8% in 2025, according to official report
summary: The Data Protection Commission reports 19,417 cases of information leaks, a decrease from the previous year.

title: Nikkei falls more than 1,400 points dragged down by South Korean stock market crash and semiconductor sell-offs
summary: The Japanese benchmark index suffers its second consecutive decline, standing at 68,256 yen in a context of global fears.

title: Portugal eliminated from World Cup after failing to score, and Cristiano Ronaldo bids farewell in tears
summary: The 41-year-old Portuguese star stated that he 'leaves with a clear conscience' after the match in which his team failed to score.

title: Mutilated remains of a 45-year-old Chinese woman appear near Yokohama port
summary: Police investigate the discovery of a torso and a head that were separated, pointing to a deliberate homicide with dismemberment.

title: Man suffers facial injuries after being attacked by a bear and falling down a ravine in Hinohara mountain, Tokyo
summary: The hiker was surprised by a brown bear and, while trying to flee, slipped and fell, sustaining injuries.

title: Government and parties agree to draw up roadmap to review medical co-payment for elderly
summary: The goal is to achieve a 'fair burden regardless of age', with a concrete plan expected by the end of the year.

title: Nominal wages in Japan grow more than 3% for longest period since 1992, while real wages rise 1.4%
summary: May data shows improvement in workers' incomes, although consumption remains weak.

title: Two Chinese coast guard vessels enter Japanese territorial waters near Senkaku Islands
summary: The armed ships were detected in the disputed area, so the Japan Coast Guard demanded they leave the area.

title: China and Russia begin joint naval exercises in the Pacific in possible warning to Japan
summary: The maneuvers include ocean patrols, which Tokyo considers a show of force amid rising regional tensions.
